page: Amazon Web Services c9gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton processors utilizing the arm64 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, this server delivers dedicated, non-hyperthreaded compute resources for workloads requiring consistent CPU execution.

The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, which offers high-speed, low-latency local data access. This hardware configuration does not include any dedicated GPUs.

Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Given the combination of dedicated arm64 compute cores, a 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, and fast local NVMe storage, this instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from local scratch space. Suitable applications include distributed data processing, batch processing, ad serving, and high-performance web servers.
description: Amazon Web Services c9gd.4xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume. It provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and does not contain any graphics processing units. This resource profile makes the instance highly efficient for compute-heavy tasks that require fast local storage, such as batch processing, distributed analytics, and web serving. The dedicated arm64 architecture offers a cost-effective alternative to traditional x86 platforms for compatible software stacks.
